sourceMovie.title: Body of Lies
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Body of Lies (2008) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Ridley Scott's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Ultimatum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Body of Lies clicked for you, The Bourne Ultimatum (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Mesrine: Killer Instinct
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Body of Lies clicked for you, Mesrine: Killer Instinct (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Body of Lies?
faq.answer: Try The Bourne Ultimatum, Mesrine: Killer Instinct, Beirut—each shares DNA with Body of Lies while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Body of Lies?
faq.answer: Body of Lies (2008) is best described as Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Body of Lies worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
